questions.text: The replacement of hydrogen atom(s) in a hydrocarbon by halogen atom(s) results in the formation of:
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides (haloalkanes) are formed from aliphatic hydrocarbons.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest boiling point?
questions.explanation: Boiling point increases with the increase in size and mass of the halogen atom due to stronger van der Waals forces.

questions.text: The SN2 reaction mechanism follows ________ kinetics.
questions.explanation: The rate depends on the concentration of both the alkyl halide and the nucleophile.

questions.text: Which alkyl halide is most reactive towards SN2 reaction?
questions.explanation: Primary halides have the least steric hindrance, allowing the nucleophile to attack easily.

questions.text: SN1 reactions are favored by:
questions.explanation: Polar protic solvents like water or alcohol stabilize the carbocation intermediate.

questions.text: Racemisation occurs in which type of reaction?
questions.explanation: The planar carbocation intermediate in SN1 allows attack from both sides.

questions.text: The order of reactivity of alcohols with a given haloacid is:
questions.explanation: Tertiary alcohols form more stable carbocations.

questions.text: Finkelstein reaction is used for the preparation of:
questions.explanation: It involves the reaction of alkyl chloride/bromide with NaI in dry acetone.

questions.text: Swarts reaction is used to prepare:
questions.explanation: Metallic fluorides like AgF or Hg2F2 are used to exchange halogens.

questions.text: Reaction of an alkyl halide with sodium in dry ether to give a higher alkane is:
questions.explanation: 2RX + 2Na -> R-R + 2NaX.

questions.text: The C-X bond in haloarenes is ________ than in haloalkanes.
questions.explanation: Due to resonance and sp2 hybridization of carbon in haloarenes.

questions.text: Which of the following is a chiral molecule?
questions.explanation: 2-Chlorobutane has a carbon atom attached to four different groups.

questions.text: The correct order of density for haloalkanes is:
questions.explanation: Density increases with the increase in atomic mass of the halogen.

questions.text: What is the hybridisation of carbon attached to halogen in vinyl halide?
questions.explanation: In vinyl halides (CH2=CH-X), the carbon is part of a double bond.

questions.text: Chlorination of benzene in presence of FeCl3 gives chlorobenzene. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: The Cl+ electrophile attacks the electron-rich benzene ring.

questions.text: Grignard reagent is prepared by the reaction of alkyl halide with ________ in dry ether.
questions.explanation: R-X + Mg -> R-Mg-X.

questions.text: Ambident nucleophiles like KCN and AgCN give different products with alkyl halides because:
options: KCN is ionic, AgCN is covalent
KCN is covalent, AgCN is ionic
questions.explanation: KCN gives nitriles (RCN) while AgCN gives isonitriles (RNC).

questions.text: Which of the following is an example of a gem-dihalide?
questions.explanation: Gem-dihalides have two halogen atoms on the same carbon atom.

questions.text: The reaction of chlorobenzene with sodium in presence of dry ether to give diphenyl is:
questions.explanation: Fitting reaction involves two aryl groups coupling.

questions.text: DDT is an effective:
questions.explanation: DDT (p,p'-Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane) was widely used to kill mosquitoes.

questions.text: Allylic halides are those in which halogen is attached to an sp3 carbon next to a:
questions.explanation: Example: CH2=CH-CH2Cl.

questions.text: Which compound shows optical isomerism?
questions.explanation: sec-Butyl chloride (2-chlorobutane) has a chiral center.

questions.text: The product of reaction between ethyl bromide and alcoholic KOH is:
questions.explanation: Alcoholic KOH causes dehydrohalogenation (elimination).

questions.text: Haloalkanes are insoluble in water because:
options: They cannot form hydrogen bonds with water
questions.explanation: The energy released during new interactions is not enough to break existing H-bonds in water.

questions.text: Phosgene is formed when ________ is exposed to air and light.
questions.explanation: Chloroform (CHCl3) oxidizes to toxic COCl2 (carbonyl chloride).

questions.text: The correct order of reactivity of alkyl halides towards SN1 is:
questions.explanation: SN1 reactivity depends on the stability of the carbocation (3° is most stable).

questions.text: Lucas reagent is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Used to distinguish between 1°, 2°, and 3° alcohols.

questions.text: Which of the following is most reactive towards nucleophilic substitution?
questions.explanation: Electron withdrawing groups (-NO2) at ortho and para positions increase reactivity of haloarenes.

questions.text: Saytzeff rule helps in predicting the major product in:
questions.explanation: It states that the more highly substituted alkene is the preferred product.

questions.text: Carbon tetrachloride is used as a:
questions.explanation: It is sold under the name Pyrene for fire extinguishing.

questions.text: The catalyst used in the Sandmeyer reaction is:
questions.explanation: Sandmeyer reaction uses Cu2Cl2 or Cu2Br2 to introduce halogens into the benzene ring.

questions.text: Which of the following is Freon-12?
questions.explanation: Dichlorodifluoromethane is a common refrigerant.

questions.text: Iodoform test is given by:
questions.explanation: Ethanol can be oxidized to acetaldehyde which contains the CH3CO- group.

questions.text: AgNO3 gives a yellow precipitate with which of the following?
questions.explanation: Silver iodide (AgI) is a yellow precipitate.

questions.text: The reaction of an alkyl halide with ammonia gives:
questions.explanation: This is Hoffmann's ammonolysis of alkyl halides.

questions.text: Which statement about SN2 is FALSE?
questions.explanation: Carbocation is formed in SN1, not SN2.

questions.text: What is the order of reactivity of halogens in free radical halogenation of alkanes?
questions.explanation: Fluorination is explosive while iodination is very slow and reversible.

questions.text: Reaction of bromoethane with aqueous KOH gives:
questions.explanation: Aqueous KOH favors nucleophilic substitution over elimination.

questions.text: Vicinal dihalides have two halogen atoms on:
questions.explanation: Example: 1,2-dichloroethane.

questions.text: Which of the following does not have a dipole moment?
questions.explanation: CCl4 is perfectly symmetrical (tetrahedral), so individual C-Cl bond moments cancel out.
